aiReadyLines.text: Notice that sometimes the answer to "Where do you come from?" or
"Where are you from?" is the name of the country one belongs to. But
when both the speakers are from the same country, the question would
obviously mean what part of the country one belongs to.
doing?” do vou do?' means the same as ‘What's your profession?’ On
the other hand, are you doing' refers to what you are doing at the
moment, or something you are doing around that time.
each person's profession is, and what he or she is doing at the moment.
What's his/her name? What does he/she do?
He/she is not . (not doing what he/she usually does, e.g.
He is not seeing a patient)
Rashid Ahmed, Doctor Sumana Chowdhury, Lawyer

rawText:
Language Focus
Notice that sometimes the answer to "Where do you come from?" or
"Where are you from?" is the name of the country one belongs to. But
when both the speakers are from the same country, the question would
obviously mean what part of the country one belongs to.
Notice the difference between "What do vou do?" and "What are you
doing?” do vou do?' means the same as ‘What's your profession?’ On
the other hand, are you doing' refers to what you are doing at the
moment, or something you are doing around that time.
Look at the pictures below. Ask and answer questions about what
each person's profession is, and what he or she is doing at the moment.
Follow this model:
What's his/her name? What does he/she do?
What is he/she doing now?
At the moment he/she is
He/she is not . (not doing what he/she usually does, e.g.
He is not seeing a patient)
Rashid Ahmed, Doctor Sumana Chowdhury, Lawyer
cleanText:
Language Focus
Notice that sometimes the answer to "Where do you come from?" or
"Where are you from?" is the name of the country one belongs to. But
when both the speakers are from the same country, the question would
obviously mean what part of the country one belongs to.
Notice the difference between "What do vou do?" and "What are you
doing?” do vou do?' means the same as ‘What's your profession?’ On
the other hand, are you doing' refers to what you are doing at the
moment, or something you are doing around that time.
Look at the pictures below. Ask and answer questions about what
each person's profession is, and what he or she is doing at the moment.
Follow this model:
What's his/her name? What does he/she do?
What is he/she doing now?
At the moment he/she is
He/she is not . (not doing what he/she usually does, e.g.
He is not seeing a patient)
Rashid Ahmed, Doctor Sumana Chowdhury, Lawyer
